source: Learning Ansible with Rocky - Ansible Basics: Configuration files and the inventory

questions.q: Where does the Ansible server configuration live?
questions.why: Under /etc/ansible you find ansible.cfg and the hosts inventory.

questions.q: What are the two main configuration files under /etc/ansible?
questions.why: ansible.cfg holds commands/modules/plugins/ssh config; hosts declares clients and groups.

questions.q: After a pip install there is no ansible.cfg. How do you create an initial one?
questions.why: ansible-config init creates a starter config; redirect it to /etc/ansible/ansible.cfg.

questions.q: What does the --disabled option of `ansible-config init` do?
options: Comments out every option by prefixing it with a ;
questions.why: --disabled comments the whole set of options with a leading ;.

questions.q: In which order does Ansible load its configuration (first match wins)?
questions.why: Env var $ANSIBLE_CONFIG, then ansible.cfg in the current dir, then ~/.ansible.cfg, else the default.

questions.q: In an INI inventory, how is a group of hosts declared?
questions.why: Groups are delimited by [header] elements; hosts belonging to them follow.

questions.q: What does the :children attribute mean, as in [linux:children]?
options: The parent group is composed of sub-groups
It lists the group's child processes
The group is restricted to child nodes only
The hosts in it are unmanaged
questions.why: A :children group is built from other groups named beneath it.

questions.q: The inventory pattern www[001:006].example.com expands to...
options: one literal host named with the brackets
questions.why: Bracketed ranges expand numerically; leading zeros are preserved.
